source: CIS AMAZON ELASTIC KUBERNETES SERVICE (EKS) BENCHMARK V1.6.0
title: Manage Kubernetes RBAC users with AWS IAM Authenticator for Kubernetes or Upgrade to AWS CLI v1.16.156 or greater

input_row.source: CIS AMAZON ELASTIC KUBERNETES SERVICE (EKS) BENCHMARK V1.6.0
input_row.title: Manage Kubernetes RBAC users with AWS IAM Authenticator for Kubernetes or Upgrade to AWS CLI v1.16.156 or greater
input_row.description: Amazon EKS uses IAM to provide authentication to your Kubernetes cluster through the AWS IAM Authenticator for Kubernetes. You can configure the stock kubectl client to work with Amazon EKS by installing the AWS IAM Authenticator for Kubernetes and modifying your kubectl configuration file to use it for authentication.
input_row.rationale: On- and off-boarding users is often difficult to automate and prone to error. Using a single source of truth for user permissions reduces the number of locations that an individual must be off-boarded from, and prevents users gaining unique permissions sets that increase the cost of audit. Impact: Users must now be assigned to the IAM group created to use this namespace and deploy applications. If they are not they will not be able to access the namespace or deploy.
input_row.audit: To Audit access to the namespace $NAMESPACE, assume the IAM role yourIAMRoleName for a user that you created, and then run the following command: $ kubectl get role -n $NAMESPACE The response lists the RBAC role that has access to this Namespace.
input_row.remediation: Refer to the 'Managing users or IAM roles for your cluster' in Amazon EKS documentation. Note: If using AWS CLI version 1.16.156 or later there is no need to install the AWS IAM Authenticator anymore. The relevant AWS CLI commands, depending on the use case, are: aws eks update-kubeconfig aws eks get-token Default Value: For role-based access control (RBAC), system:masters permissions are configured in the Amazon EKS control plane References: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/eks/latest/userguide/add-user-role.html 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/eks/latest/userguide/add-user-role.html
input_row.impact: Users must now be assigned to the IAM group created to use this namespace and deploy applications. If they are not they will not be able to access the namespace or deploy.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_eks_cluster_iam_authenticator_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

To ensure compliance with the given control, the audit process will incorporate the following steps:

1. **AWS CLI Commands:**
   - Utilize the `aws eks update-kubeconfig` and `aws eks get-token` commands to verify that the EKS cluster is correctly configured with the AWS IAM Authenticator for Kubernetes. These commands help ensure that the AWS CLI version is 1.16.156 or greater.

2. **boto3 SDK Methods:**
   - Use the `boto3` EKS client to list all EKS clusters in the region using `list_clusters()`. For each cluster, verify the IAM roles associated with it and check the authentication configuration.
   - Specifically, use `describe_cluster()` for each cluster to review its configuration, focusing on the IAM roles and their policies to ensure they include permissions needed for the AWS IAM Authenticator.

3. **Configuration Validation:**
   - Ensure that users or IAM roles intended to authenticate with the cluster are listed in the cluster’s authentication configuration. Check configuration settings to verify the IAM Authenticator is properly set up.
   - Validate that IAM roles tied to the cluster have the necessary permissions outlined in the provided references.

4. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - **Pass:** If all clusters have appropriate IAM roles configured, with AWS CLI version 1.16.156 or greater used in conjunction with the IAM Authenticator or modern `aws eks` commands, reflecting the proper configuration.
   - **Fail:** If any cluster lacks the correct IAM configurations or requires older methods of authentication (pre-CLI v1.16.156) without the modern enhancements available post-upgrade.

By systematically applying these API queries and command checks, the control can be audited efficiently and effectively, ensuring that Kubernetes RBAC users are managed correctly via AWS IAM and that any outdated authentication methods are flagged for remediation.
